insights.title: Notes choke across groups, not within them - polyphonic play inside a group is allowed
insights.summary: Triggering a note in group N kills voices from OTHER processors in group N. Multiple notes in the same group can play simultaneously; only a new incoming note triggers a choke. This is correct drum behavior: playing two open hi-hats does not self-choke.

insights.title: Built-in processor does not support dynamic per-note group assignment
insights.summary: The ChokeGroup parameter applies to the whole processor, not to individual MIDI notes. Drum machines that need to assign any of N notes to any of M groups at runtime cannot use the built-in processor and must implement choke logic in a ScriptProcessor using event IDs.

insights.title: Each drum type (e.g. open hat, closed hat, kick) needs its own sampler with a ChokeGroupProcessor
insights.summary: Because the processor manages voices on its owner synth only, the standard setup is one sampler per drum type, each with a ChokeGroupProcessor set to the appropriate group number. Processors on different samplers with the same group number will choke each other globally.

insights.title: Original motivation: MIDI player multichannel setups where scripts cannot see cross-channel events
insights.summary: When MIDI players send notes into separate channels, a ScriptProcessor on one channel never sees note-on events on other channels, making script-based choke unreliable. The built-in ChokeGroupProcessor uses a global registry inside EventIdHandler that works across the entire module tree regardless of channel routing.

insights.title: Hi/LoKey range also blocks all-notes-off behavior and is not a standalone note filter
insights.summary: Users wanting a simple note range filter are often misled by the LoKey/HiKey parameters. These parameters are coupled to choke group logic and are not equivalent to a standalone MIDI range filter. For range filtering without choke behavior, a dedicated script or a different processor is needed.

insights.title: Manual choke via script requires Message.makeArtificial() before storing event IDs
insights.summary: When implementing choke logic in a ScriptProcessor (pre-built processor approach, or for dynamic group assignment), you must call Message.makeArtificial() on note-ons before pushing their event IDs to an array. HISE can only kill artificial events via Synth.noteOffByEventId() for stability reasons.

insights.title: Script-based choke for dynamic group membership: track a rolling evtList per group
insights.summary: For drum machines where notes can be reassigned to groups at runtime, maintain one array per choke group. On note-on for a choke group member, call Synth.noteOffByEventId() for all IDs in that group's array, clear it, then push the new artificial event ID. This gives correct mono-choke behavior for any combination of notes.
